name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans up to $500, installment loans up to $1,000, and title loans up to $25,000 at 1,400+ US locations with same-day funding.
description_long:
Advance America is one of the largest consumer lending chains in the United States, operating over 1,400 brick-and-mortar locations nationwide. Founded in 1997, it serves millions of customers annually and is accredited by the Community Financial Services Association of America (CFSA). The Jacksonville location on N. Edgewood Ave. serves the Commonwealth Village neighborhood west of downtown and is one of many Florida storefronts in the network.

The company offers three core short-term loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are structured as two-to-four-week loans meant to bridge gaps between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment across multiple payments over a longer term. Title Loans from $2,000 to $25,000 are available using a paid-off vehicle as collateral, offered in partnership with LoanCenter. All three products are available both online and in-store, and Western Union money transfer services are also offered at this location.

What sets Advance America apart from smaller regional operators is its scale and customer service infrastructure. The company holds 4.9 out of 5 stars across over 125,401 verified reviews, with more than 200,000 reviews on Google nationally. The Jacksonville branch offers bilingual English/Spanish service, and applicants can pre-qualify by phone before visiting the store. Applications require a government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN.

Advance America is a legitimate, regulated lender suitable for consumers facing genuine short-term cash gaps who have exhausted lower-cost options. However, payday and installment loans of this type carry very high annualized interest rates — often 300–400% APR or more — and title loans put the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession if they default. These products are expensive by design and should be treated as a last resort, not a routine financial tool. Borrowers who qualify for a credit union payday alternative loan (PAL) or an employer advance will almost always pay less.
address: 7932 N Oak Trafficway Ste. 204, Kansas City, MO 64118

company_info.headquarters: 7932 N Oak Trafficway Ste. 204, Kansas City, MO 64118

pros: 4.9/5 star rating across 125,401+ verified customer reviews
Three loan types under one roof — payday, installment, and title loans
Title loans up to $25,000 for borrowers with a paid-off vehicle
Online and in-store application options with phone pre-qualification
Bilingual English/Spanish service available at this location
Same-day funding available for approved loans
Nationally accredited company with 1,400+ physical locations
cons: Payday loans cap at $500 — insufficient for larger emergencies
Payday loan terms (2–4 weeks) and typical APRs make repeat borrowing very costly
Title loans use your car as collateral — default risks vehicle repossession
Title loans are through third-party LoanCenter, not directly Advance America
Closed Sundays — limits access for weekend emergencies requiring in-person service
best_for: Employed adults who need $100–$500 fast and can repay within two to four weeks
Borrowers needing a small installment loan with structured repayments up to $1,000
Car owners with a paid-off vehicle needing $2,000–$25,000 with no credit requirement
Spanish-speaking consumers who prefer in-person bilingual loan assistance
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for employed borrowers who need quick cash up to $1,000 and can repay within a few weeks or payment cycles. The main caveat is cost — these are among the most expensive loan products available, and title loans carry the additional risk of losing your vehicle; consumers with any access to lower-cost credit should exhaust those options first.
services: Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000, vehicle as collateral, via LoanCenter)
